body.title: [triage] HealthMonitor calls a SATURATED inference source HEALTHY — it probes reachability (/api/tags 200), which is exactly the property that stays true while capacity is zero. Routing trusts this.
body.intention: I intend that a source's health signal reflects whether it can DO THE JOB, so that routing never sends work to a source that answers the phone and nothing else. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
**SPLIT OUT of T-default-suite-drives-live-inference-and-saturates-hex (closed proven).**
That ticket fixed the TEST side — live tests are now opt-in, and their admission guard
(`tests/support/live_guard.can_infer`) does a real one-token completion instead of a TCP connect.
**This ticket is the DEVICE side, which is untouched and still wrong.**

`HealthMonitor` (unseen_university/devices/inference/health_monitor.py) pings each source via
`source.ping()` on a daemon thread. `OllamaSource.ping()` probes **`GET /api/tags`** — and on
2026-07-13, while Hex's single inference slot (`-np 1`) was hours deep and no completion could
return, `/api/tags` kept answering **200 in 300µs**, because it is served by the Go parent and never
touches the slot. **HealthMonitor called that source HEALTHY the entire time, and the routing
selector trusted it.**

The bitter part: `tests/inference/test_ollama_liveness.py` exists *specifically* to kill this bug. It
upgraded `ping()` from a bare socket-connect to `GET /api/tags`. **Both are reachability.** The fix
moved the goalpost without leaving the field. **REACHABILITY IS EXACTLY THE PROPERTY THAT STAYS TRUE
WHILE CAPACITY IS ZERO.**

**⚠ A REAL DESIGN TENSION — DO NOT SKIP THIS, IT IS WHY THE TICKET WAS SPLIT RATHER THAN JUST DONE.**
The obvious fix — make `ping()` do a tiny real completion (`num_predict:1`) — is **not obviously
right**, because HealthMonitor runs it **on a timer, for every source**. A periodic capability probe
**adds load to the very slot whose scarcity it is measuring.** On a single-slot host under contention,
the probe joins the queue it is trying to detect. (This is exactly why the *test* guard was safe: it
runs ONCE at collection, not on a timer.) The implementer must resolve this, not paper over it.
Candidate directions, none yet chosen:
  * a capability probe with a SHORT timeout, run at a LOW frequency, accepting that it queues (a
    timed-out probe correctly reports DOWN — the added load is one tiny abandoned request per tick);
  * infer capacity from data the host already exposes (`/api/ps` shows resident models; llama-server
    exposes slot state) without submitting work;
  * treat OBSERVED completion latency from real traffic as the health signal — the calls we are
    already making are a free, perfectly-representative probe, and the request that times out is the
    ground truth. (This is the most promising: it costs nothing and cannot lie.)
  * a circuit breaker: N consecutive timeouts -> DOWN, without any probe at all.

**Test plan / discriminating fixture:** a fake source that answers `/api/tags` 200 but never completes
`/api/chat` must be reported **DOWN**; one that answers both is **UP**. A probe that checks only
reachability passes BOTH and fails this fixture — that is the whole point. Plus: whatever mechanism is
chosen must be shown NOT to add material load to a saturated host (state the added requests/tick).

**Completion criteria:**
  1. A source that serves metadata but cannot complete is reported DOWN (fixture above).
  2. The routing selector does not choose such a source.
  3. The design tension is RESOLVED and the resolution is stated — how much load the health signal
     adds to a contended host, and why that is acceptable.
  4. `test_ollama_liveness.py` is extended so the reachability-only probe can never come back.
